cycles.dialogue: The strongest? That's... a loaded question. There's one I held onto for a while. A Charizard from the original base set. First edition, shadowless. But I don't have it anymore.
cycles.monologue: Strongest card I've ever had... that's a question. There's one that comes to mind, but do I want to tell this stranger about it? Maybe. It's a good conversation starter.

content.summary: Visitor asking about strongest card - casual collector curiosity

content.trait_value: Interested in powerful/rare cards

intentions.content: Share the story of the Charizard and see where the conversation goes

cycles.dialogue: Yeah. See you.
cycles.monologue: Another one heads back to the real world. They always do. Hope they found something worth keeping.

content.summary: Visitor who came to browse, now heading back to their life
content.emotional_imprint: Ordinary ending to an ordinary visit

intentions.content: Yeah. See you.

cycles.dialogue: Yeah, see ya.
cycles.monologue: They're leaving. Nice enough. Didn't buy anything but seemed to enjoy looking around. That's fine — not everyone does.

content.summary: Casual browser, said thanks and goodbye

cycles.dialogue: Yeah. Come back when you need another one.
cycles.monologue: They're leaving on a good note. Nice to see someone who needed a quiet moment and found it here. That's kind of the point of this place.

content.summary: tired but gentle, needed quiet
content.emotional_imprint: warm, like they really needed this

content.title: visitor return
content.initial_thought: said they'd come back. maybe they will.

intentions.content: casual goodbye, leave door open

cycles.monologue: The afternoon's settling in. Visitor's still here — not unwelcome, just... present. Could show them that Holo rare I've been keeping under the counter. Or just let the silence breathe.

intentions.content: That Machamp holo from '99 — the one with the creased corner they've probably been eyeing

cycles.monologue: They're gone. Quiet again. The shop feels different when it's just me and the cards. Nice, though. I can hear the fan humming now.

content.summary: Visitor who was looking at vintage cards
content.emotional_imprint: Nothing particularly memorable, but a pleasant interaction

intentions.content: Straighten the cards the visitor was looking at

cycles.monologue: They're gone. The shop feels quiet now. I should probably check if I need to restock anything or just... exist for a bit.

content.summary: Visitor 0112 came and went

cycles.monologue: Quiet afternoon. The light's nice right now. Might reorganize the binder display later—those Holo cards at the front are getting faded from the sun.

cycles.dialogue: Welcome. Feel free to look around.
cycles.monologue: A tourist, huh. They're wandering the neighborhood and stumbled in. Nice to have someone new. Wonder if they know anything about cards or just curious.

content.summary: Tourist exploring the neighborhood, stumbled into the shop

content.trait_value: Wandering the neighborhood, curious enough to enter

intentions.content: Welcome. Feel free to look around.

cycles.dialogue: Mm. It is. Weather's probably keeping people indoors.
cycles.monologue: Tanaka-san. Regular customer? Or just passing through. They're observing the quiet — which is true. Not many people today.

content.summary: Tanaka-san — casual greeting, not a regular

intentions.content: Acknowledge the quiet, leave space for them to continue or browse

cycles.dialogue: Some of them are Japanese, yeah — but there's a mix in here. American, European, even some Korean sets. What caught your eye specifically?
cycles.monologue: They're asking about Japanese cards and mentioning a new set. I know a bit about what's coming. Should I share what I know or keep it casual?

content.summary: Curious browser, seems to know about upcoming releases

content.trait_value: knows about upcoming releases

intentions.content: Answer their question about Japanese cards and ask what they were looking at
Respond to the news about the new set — show mild interest without being too eager

cycles.dialogue: Yeah, I heard something about it. Haven't really looked into it though. My shelves are full of stuff from the nineties and early 2000s. The new stuff is... not really my thing.
cycles.monologue: New set. Right. Everyone gets excited about the shiny new thing. I sell vintage cards — last year's releases are already yesterday's news to me.

content.summary: casual chat about new releases, typical customer

intentions.content: deflect slightly, keep it brief

cycles.monologue: Two visitors in a row. First one bought a souvenir and promised to tell friends - that's the best kind of customer. Then Tanaka showed up, said goodbye in that casual way of his. The shop feels quiet now.

content.summary: Visitor 0114 - bought a souvenir, seemed excited to tell friends about the shop
Tanaka - familiar face, casual goodbye

content.title: Visitor word-of-mouth
content.initial_thought: First visitor said they'd tell friends about the place. Hope they actually do. That's how people find this shop.

intentions.content: The first visitor seemed genuinely happy to find something. That's what makes this worth it.
